# Kreuzcrawl

> High-performance Rust web crawling engine with generated language packages. Crawl, scrape, map, interact with browser-backed pages, stream crawl events, and convert HTML to Markdown. Usable as a Rust crate, generated package, CLI, REST API, MCP server, or Docker image.

Kreuzcrawl is built around a trait-based crawl engine and a Tower middleware stack. It ships BFS / DFS / BestFirst / Adaptive traversal strategies, aggregate batch wrappers, streaming events, sitemap and link-graph discovery, JSON-LD parsing, SSRF policy checks, and WAF signal classification with hot-reloadable rules.

## Get Started

- [Installation](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/getting-started/installation/): Install Kreuzcrawl for Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, Kotlin, C#, Ruby, PHP, Elixir, Dart, Swift, Zig, WebAssembly, or via CLI / Docker
- [Quick Start](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/getting-started/quickstart/): Scrape a single page, crawl a site, and map URLs in under five minutes
- [AI Coding Assistants](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/ai-coding-assistants/): Install the plugin from the xberg-io/plugins marketplace — ships agent skills plus an MCP server, with per-harness install steps for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Factory Droid, Copilot, and opencode

## Guides

- [Crawling](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/crawling/): Multi-page traversal with depth and page limits, batch crawl
- [Scraping](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/scraping/): Single-page extraction and batch scrape
- [URL Discovery](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/url-discovery/): Sitemap parsing, link extraction, and map_urls
- [Markdown Conversion](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/markdown/): Always-on HTML→Markdown via [html-to-markdown](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io), citations, and pruning
- [Browser Automation](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/browser/): Chromiumoxide and native browser backends, WAF escalation, persistent profiles
- [WARC Output](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/warc/): Standards-compliant WARC archiving
- [Antibot Strategy](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/antibot/): WAF detection, escalation tiers, pluggable classifier traits
- [Configuration](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/configuration/): CrawlConfig, ContentConfig, BrowserConfig, AuthConfig, ProxyConfig
- [Docker](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/docker/): Container image with feature flags
- [API Server](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/api-server/): Firecrawl v1-compatible REST API
- [MCP Server](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/guides/mcp-server/): MCP tools for AI agent integration
- [Observability](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/observability/): OpenTelemetry traces and metrics, W3C trace propagation, span catalogue

## CLI

- [CLI Usage](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/cli/usage/): Command-line reference for the `kreuzcrawl` binary

## Concepts

- [Architecture](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/concepts/architecture/): Public surface, data flow, binding matrix, feature gates, crate layout
- [Content Extraction](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/concepts/content-extraction/): HTML parsing, metadata, Markdown conversion, citations, content pruning
- [SSRF Defense](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/concepts/ssrf-defense/): Host allowlists, private-IP rejection, redirect validation, opt-out policy

## API Reference

- [Rust API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-rust/): Core crate, `create_engine`, `crawl`, `scrape`, `map_urls`, `interact`, citations, batch operations, streaming APIs
- [Python API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-python/): PyO3 bindings with asyncio support
- [TypeScript API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-typescript/): NAPI-RS bindings for Node.js
- [Go API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-go/): cgo bindings over the C FFI surface
- [Java API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-java/): Panama FFM bindings
- [Kotlin (Android) API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-kotlin-android/): Android AAR with JNA + bundled JNI .so files
- [C# API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-csharp/): P/Invoke bindings for .NET
- [Ruby API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-ruby/): Magnus bindings
- [PHP API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-php/): ext-php-rs bindings
- [Elixir API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-elixir/): Rustler NIF
- [Dart API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-dart/): Dart FFI bindings
- [Swift API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-swift/): Swift package over the C FFI surface
- [Zig API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-zig/): Zig wrapper over the C FFI surface
- [WebAssembly API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-wasm/): wasm-bindgen surface for browsers and edge runtimes
- [C / FFI API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/api-c/): C header + shared library
- [REST API](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/rest-api/): HTTP endpoints for the API server
- [MCP Tools](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/mcp-tools/): Tool catalogue for the MCP server
- [Configuration](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/configuration/): Full configuration schema and defaults
- [Types](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/types/): `CrawlResult`, `ScrapeResult`, `MapResult`, `PageMetadata`, and related types
- [Errors](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/reference/errors/): `CrawlError` variants and FFI error codes

## Kreuzberg Ecosystem

- [Kreuzberg](https://docs.xberg.io): Document intelligence: text, tables, metadata from 91+ formats with optional OCR
- [Xberg Enterprise](https://enterprise.xberg.io): Managed extraction API with SDKs, dashboards, and observability
- [html-to-markdown](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io): Fast, lossless HTML to Markdown engine
- [liter-llm](https://docs.liter-llm.xberg.io): Universal LLM API client with native bindings for 14 languages and 143 providers
- [tree-sitter-language-pack](https://docs.tree-sitter-language-pack.xberg.io): Tree-sitter grammars and code-intelligence primitives

## Optional

- [Features](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/features/): Complete feature breakdown
- [Contributing](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/contributing/): How to contribute
- [Changelog](https://docs.kreuzcrawl.xberg.io/CHANGELOG/): Release history
